CurrentIssues.ID: LoopWithTooManyJumpStatements:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$while
MagicNumber:CrashHandler.kt$CrashHandler$10
MagicNumber:LogStore.kt$LogStore$1024
MagicNumber:LogStore.kt$LogStore$256L
MagicNumber:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$1_000
MagicNumber:Polling.kt$5
MagicNumber:Polling.kt$60_000L
MatchingDeclarationName:AppLogPoll.kt$PollLogState
MaxLineLength:AppLog.kt$AppLog$/** Diagnostics gate. OFF ŌåÆ [v]/[d] are no-ops (normal run is quiet, capture stays small); ON ŌåÆ per-frame / lifecycle seams (WS, Repo, Net, VM, Life) light up. */

MaxLineLength:AppLog.kt$AppLog$/** Logging facade over [android.util.Log]. Lines land in logcat under our pid; [LogcatCollector] mirrors them to a file. [v]/[d] gated by [verbose]; [i]/[w]/[e] always fire. */

MaxLineLength:AppLogCatch.kt$/** Run [block], log the caught exception at [level] ('w' default, 'e' or 'd'), and return [fallback]. For silent catches that should still record into the captured log. */
MaxLineLength:AppLogPoll.kt$PollLogState$/** Polling accumulator: logs ONCE on first failure, then ONCE on recovery. Silent in steady state ŌĆö avoids per-tick noise. */

MaxLineLength:CrashHandler.kt$CrashHandler$/** Catches uncaught exceptions on any thread, writes a crash report via [LogStore], then chains to the previous handler so the OS still shows its dialog / kills the process. Install once, as early as possible (from [dev.agentic.AgenticApp.onCreate]). */
MaxLineLength:CrashHandler.kt$CrashHandler$try { store.writeCrash(thread, error) } catch (e: Throwable) { AppLog.e("App", "CrashHandler.writeCrash failed", e) }

MaxLineLength:LogNoise.kt$LogNoise$/** Line filter for [LogcatCollector]: drops known framework spam (e.g. Samsung `setRequestedFrameRate` per Compose draw) before it hits the capture file so the rotation budget holds hours instead of seconds. Keep patterns NARROW (message substrings, not whole tags) ŌĆö blanket tag drops would discard genuine View warnings/errors a crash run-up might need. */
MaxLineLength:LogNoiseTest.kt$LogNoiseTest$// LogNoise drops framework spam (e.g. Samsung Android 16's 92.5% setRequestedFrameRate INFO spam) before it hits the capture file.

MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore$/** Mark all current crashes as seen so the next-launch prompt doesn't re-fire. Anchors the marker to the newest crash file's mtime (not the wall clock) so a backward clock jump between crash and ack can't make a seen crash look unseen again. */

MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore$/** Owns the on-disk logs dir: rolling logcat capture, crash reports, zip export, tail viewer, clear, seen-state. App-private storage (no permission); [clear] must be called while the collector is stopped to avoid racing its open writer. */
MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore$/** Tail (last [maxBytes]) of the active capture file. Tails rather than loads whole file so multi-MB logs don't blow Compose / memory. */
MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore$/** Verbose-logging gate (drives [AppLog.verbose] and the logcatŌåÆfile capture). Crash reports write independently and are never lost when this is off. */
MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore$/** Writes a standalone crash report and prunes old ones. Best-effort, never throws (runs inside the uncaught-exception handler). */

MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore$/** Zips the logs dir (capture + rotations + crashes) plus a device-info header into cacheDir/log-exports. Old exports deleted first so the share sheet never offers a stale file. */
MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore$// closeEntry() in finally: if the copy throws (disk full, concurrent change), the entry is still closed so the stream stays valid and remaining files still zip.
MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore$appendLine("app: ${context.packageName} ${pkg?.versionName ?: "?"} (build ${pkg?.let { versionCodeOf(it) } ?: "?"})")
MaxLineLength:LogStore.kt$LogStore.Companion$// SimpleDateFormat is not thread-safe; build a fresh instance per call. Locale.US keeps timestamps stable regardless of device locale.

MaxLineLength:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$/** Streams this process's logcat into [LogStore.logFile] with size rotation. At most one worker thread + one logcat subprocess at a time; [stop] joins before returning so a fast offŌåÆon toggle can't leave two writers open. Reading our own pid needs no permission; on locked-down ROMs that deny exec'ing logcat the read loop degrades quietly ŌĆö crash reports still write via [CrashHandler]. */
MaxLineLength:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$// -T 1 ŌåÆ start from the most recent line (avoid re-dumping the entire ring buffer on every (re)start); --pid ŌåÆ only our process.

MaxLineLength:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$// Always tear down on ANY exit path (stop(), EOF, or exception): clear running so a later start() can restart, destroy the subprocess so an EOF/exception exit never leaks a logcat child.
MaxLineLength:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$// Publish handle under the lock, re-checking `running` so a stop() that raced our start() tears this subprocess down instead of orphaning it.

MaxLineLength:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$// Update shared state under the lock, then join OUTSIDE the lock ŌĆö joining inside would deadlock the worker, which needs the same lock to publish its subprocess handle.

MaxLineLength:Polling.kt$/** Cold Flow: immediately emit [block], then every [intervalMs]. Cancellation propagates; other errors trigger capped-exponential backoff (max 60s) and resume the normal cadence on the next success. */
NestedBlockDepth:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$private fun runLoop()
TooGenericExceptionCaught:AppLogCatch.kt$e: Exception
TooGenericExceptionCaught:CrashHandler.kt$CrashHandler$e: Throwable
TooGenericExceptionCaught:LogcatCollector.kt$LogcatCollector$t: Throwable
TooGenericExceptionCaught:Polling.kt$e: Throwable
TooGenericExceptionThrown:PollingTest.kt$PollingTest$throw RuntimeException("boom")
